title: 3 Establishing Policies for Client Centre User Management
body_text:
3	Establishing Policies for Client Centre User Management
3.1	Policy on Restricting Access to Applications on the Client Centre
The Client Centre is the gateway to many high-security applications (e.g., the Wires application) that may be susceptible to employee fraud. We recommended that you segregate duties and restrict administrative access to applications on the Client Centre.
Your financial institution is responsible for designating its own User Management Security Officers (Security Officers). Practice a high level of due diligence when appointing Security Officers. Make sure that the applications assigned to each Security Officer and each Client Centre User reflects current job responsibilities and authorization levels.
Note:	Your financial institution must assign at least two Security Officers to add, modify, and delete Client Centre Users. When determining the number of Security Officers that you require, ensure that you consider coverage for absences.
3.2	Policy on Internal Controls to User Management and Client Centre User Management
Your financial institution is responsible for employing security practices and establishing internal controls to monitor Client Centre Users. We recommend that you establish internal controls and procedures relating to the use of applications on the Client Centre to prevent errors or to detect them promptly. Your operations staff and external and internal auditors should pay particular attention to internal controls for managing Client Centre Users.
Policies may include but aren芒聙聶t limited to the following:
Ensuring that Client Centre Users and Security Officers have their own unique user IDs.
Important
:	Avoid shared user IDs.
Deleting Client Centre Users and Security Officers who
芒聢聮
no longer require User Management or Client Centre access,
芒聢聮
are no longer employees of your financial institution, or
芒聢聮
are inactive for one year or more.
Note:	Deleting a Client Centre User ID is permanent. The deleted user ID can芒聙聶t be used again.
Ensuring that you review Client Centre Users and Security Officers regularly and that, if required, you update or delete user profiles.
If a user hasn芒聙聶t accessed the Client Centre for 13 months, Central 1 will disable the user芒聙聶s account. After a further 90 days, Central 1 will delete the user芒聙聶s account. A deleted account can be recovered for up to 90 days.
If the Client Centre User is new and doesn芒聙聶t access the Client Centre or any application via the Client Centre within 15 days following the account creation date, Central 1 will update the user芒聙聶s  account status to inactive.
3.3	Policy on Passwords
Secure passwords for Client Centre Users and Security Officers are necessary to prevent unauthorized access. A password breach could result in any of the following:
Theft of data or confidential information
Destruction of network systems
Deletion of data
Employee impersonation
Fraud
Breach of customer confidentiality
Damage to your financial institution芒聙聶s reputation
Establish policies to mitigate these risks. You can include the following guidelines in your policies:
One-time passwords must be
芒聢聮
unique for each Client Centre User and Security Officer,
芒聢聮
communicated in a secure and confidential manner, and
芒聢聮
changed by the Client Centre User or Security Officer as quickly as possible.
Characteristics of secure passwords:
芒聢聮
A minimum of eight characters.
芒聢聮
Contains alphanumeric and special characters, including capital letters.
芒聢聮
Not reusing previous passwords.
Passwords must not contain common acronyms, including financial institution name abbreviations, and words or numbers that have personal significance to the end user, such as birth dates or the names of relatives and pets.
Passwords shouldn芒聙聶t be used on other websites or computer systems. We recommend using a passphrase as they are harder to guess (e.g., 芒聙聹deskchairphonemug芒聙聺 or 芒聙聹I LOVE ice Cream day芒聙聺).
Passwords are checked against a list of known unsecure passwords to prevent the use of passwords that are known to have been exposed or shared from internet data breaches. This means that some password changes will fail even though the new password meets the structure requirements. If this occurs, the user should select a new password or use a passphrase instead.
Rules for Regular Password Updates
Passwords must be changed every six months. Client Centre Users and Security Officers should avoid reusing previous passwords. The last six passwords can芒聙聶t be reused.
Rules for Secure Password Use
To prevent a password breach, we recommend that you establish rules regarding password use, for example:
Users should memorize passwords and not write them down.
Users shouldn芒聙聶t give their password to a co-worker for any reason. This includes network operations and Help Desk staff.
Each Client Centre User must have their own login ID and password. Shared login IDs and passwords must be avoided.
Users should ensure that no one is watching when they enter their password.
Reporting of Password Breaches
If Client Centre Users or Security Officers think that their password has been compromised, they must
report the incident to their immediate supervisor, and
change their password immediately.
Ensure that Client Centre Users and Security Officers are aware that they芒聙聶re responsible for their passwords, and that they芒聙聶ll be held partly responsible for any operational misuse that occurs under their account.
END
